feed.title: Cycling Paris
feed.subtitle: Five views on cycling in Paris: routes, hidden spots, cargo bikes, gear picks and road journals — from family adventures to filmmaker rides

entry.title: Our verdict: cargo bike or trailer for your Parisian family?
entry.summary: Cargo bike or trailer? After six episodes, here is our final verdict: four Parisian family profiles, four concrete recommendations, the questions to ask before visiting a dealer, and resources to get started without getting it wrong.

entry.title: Kitting Out Your Family Cargo Bike: The Essential Accessories (And the Ones to Skip)
entry.summary: Certified seat, rain cab, legal lighting, proper lock: kitting out a family cargo bike demands careful choices. A guide to the accessories worth buying — and the ones you'll regret.

entry.title: Electric Cargo Bike for Families: Real Range, Charging, and Daily Life
entry.summary: Does electric assist really change the equation for a Parisian family? Real range figures with two children on board, the realities of flat charging, and a three-year cost breakdown — plus the models that have genuinely proved their worth.

entry.title: Bike Trailers with Children: A Complete Guide to Paris
entry.summary: Cheaper than a cargo bike and compatible with your existing bicycle, a trailer seems the obvious solution for family cycling in Paris. But the city has surprises in store: lane widths, hitches, braking, parking. A complete guide to choosing and using a trailer in the city.

entry.title: The longtail for families: compact, agile — but is it enough?
entry.summary: The longtail cargo bike promises the manoeuvrability of a regular bicycle with the capacity of a cargo. But is it really enough for a family with two children in Paris? Thomas tested the Yuba Mundo, Xtracycle Edgerunner and R&M Load 75.

entry.title: Cargo Box Bikes with Children in Paris: What Nobody Tells You
entry.summary: Cargo box bikes and children in Paris: behind the appealing image lies the width that won't fit through doorways, the braking that surprises on descents, and the storage question nobody asks before purchase. A clear-eyed look at the Babboe City, Christiania, and Nihola.

entry.title: Cargo Bike or Trailer: Asking the Right Questions Before You Choose
entry.summary: Before comparing models, ask the right questions: how many children, what terrain, what's the real budget, where will you store it? A methodical guide to choosing between a cargo bike and a trailer — clearly and without regret.

entry.title: Paris from the Saddle: What Cycling Taught Us About Our City
entry.summary: Five cyclists explored Paris by bike for months. They discovered a social geography written into the tarmac, a nocturnal city that belongs only to wheels, and seasons that change everything.

entry.title: Six Months Car-Free: What Really Changed
entry.summary: Six months after selling our car, the verdict is clear: €150 saved per month, children sleeping better, and a city rediscovered from the saddle of a cargo bike. An honest family chronicle, figures included.

entry.title: Filming from a Bike: Gear and Techniques for the Cyclist-Filmmaker
entry.summary: GoPro on the helmet, Insta360 on the handlebars, editing on CapCut: filming from a bike in Paris is a skill you can learn. Zoé, a young filmmaker documenting her Parisian rides for two years, shares her experience.

entry.title: Parking Your Cargo Bike in Paris: The Honest Guide
entry.summary: Racks too narrow, unusable cellars, insurance that rarely pays out: the reality of cargo bike parking in Paris in 2025. Six months of trial and error to find what actually works.

entry.title: Weekly Grocery Runs by Cargo Bike: Organisation and Equipment
entry.summary: Eighteen months of replacing the car with a cargo bike for the family shop. What really works, what doesn't, and how to organise weekly shopping for four with a longtail or front-loader.

entry.title: A Family Weekend by Cargo Bike: Paris to Auvers-sur-Oise
entry.summary: Paris to Auvers-sur-Oise by family cargo bike: sixty kilometres of canal towpath, open plain, and Van Gogh light. A weekend account with children, bike-friendly addresses, and everything you need to know about the train home.

entry.title: Our First Cargo Bike Ride with the Kids: What We Wish We'd Known
entry.summary: Our first cargo bike ride with Emma (4) and Théo (2): the mistakes we made, what worked well, and everything we wished we'd known. An honest account.

entry.title: Fixing Your Bike in Paris: Participatory Repair Workshops and Trusted Mechanics
entry.summary: Paris is full of participatory workshops where you can learn to fix your own bike, as well as trusted independent mechanics by neighbourhood. Punctures, cargo bikes, rare parts: a complete guide.

entry.title: The Best Parisian Cycling Routes Nobody Talks About
entry.summary: Paris hides exceptional cycle routes that even regular cyclists overlook. From the deserted Boulevard Mortier to the nocturnal charm of Rue de Charenton, Marco shares five gems that took years to uncover.

entry.title: Electric Bikes in Paris: What Really Changes
entry.summary: The e-bike is neither a gadget nor a miracle solution — but it genuinely changes cycling in Paris. An honest look at real-world range, total cost, and the models that deliver on their promises.

entry.title: Cargo Bike Hire in Paris: The Complete 2025 Guide
entry.summary: Thinking of hiring a cargo bike in Paris before you buy? Good idea — but you need to know where to go, what to pay and what questions to ask. The complete 2025 guide to cargo hire in the capital.

entry.title: Cargo Bike in Paris: Bakfiets, Tricycle or Longtail?
entry.summary: Bakfiets, tricycle or longtail: which cargo bike suits Paris life? Sophie K. compares all three types by profile — family, solo, or weekly shop — with real figures and the all-important lift question.

entry.title: Paris Along the Seine: Left Bank from Saint-Cloud to Bercy
entry.summary: From Saint-Cloud to Bercy, the left bank of the Seine offers 20 kilometres of near-continuous lower riverbank cycling — Paris's smoothest cycle route. Everything you need to know before setting off.
